content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (Jun 21, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development today focused on hardening the agent framework through comprehensive E2E coverage, critical UI/UX improvements for cloud-agent handoffs, and performance optimizations. We successfully implemented a mandatory E2E ship-gate, resolved several transient failure bugs, and refined our on-device inference strategies.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

### E2E Coverage and CI Hardening
*   Implemented a mandatory E2E coverage ship-gate, ensuring all slash commands, plugin routes, and views are verified before merging ([#8816](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8816), [#8846](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8846)).
*   Realigned and fixed stale UI-smoke test specs to match current product designs, ensuring reliable CI feedback ([#8830](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8830), [#8831](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8831), [#8834](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8834), [#8835](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8835)).

### UI/UX and Cloud Handoff Improvements
*   Enhanced chat reliability with a one-tap "Retry" feature for transient failures and improved visibility for shared-to-dedicated cloud-agent handoffs ([#8855](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8855), [#8845](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8845)).
*   Fixed legibility and rendering bugs for the cloud-handoff banner on mobile devices ([#8847](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8847)).
*   Improved error messaging for rate-limited (429) chat replies to prevent user confusion ([#8824](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8824)).

### Performance and Core Logic
*   Optimized agent cold-boot times and fixed issues where sub-agents failed to execute code properly ([#8836](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8836), [#8841](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8841)).
*   Parallelized document-recovery searches to reduce chat latency ([#8850](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8850)).
*   Refactored core logic to deduplicate session code and standardize rate-limit detection ([#8842](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8842), [#8843](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8843)).

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   [#8856](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8856): Seed dedicated-agent persona from default character.
*   [#8854](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8854): Emit `json_object` for Cerebras models.
*   [#8851](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8851): Refactor plugin-x auto-posting.
*   [#8840](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8840): CI cleanup (drop duplicate test lanes).
*   [#8822](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8822): Batch embedding drain requests.
*   [#8821](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8821): Support callback functions in character templates.

## CLOSED ISSUES

### E2E Testing and Quality Assurance
*   Completed the comprehensive E2E coverage sweep, establishing a mandatory CI ship-gate for all plugin routes and commands ([#8802](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8802)).

## NEW ISSUES

### LifeOps and Inference Optimization
*   [#8833](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8833): LifeOps split: complete live owner/agent connector and view validation.
*   [#8848](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8848): On-device inference: enforce model optimizations (TurboQuant, MTP, KV-quant) to meet performance constraints.

## ACTIVE ISSUES
*   **[#8802](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8802) E2E coverage sweep:** Successfully closed after verifying all acceptance criteria, including the transition of the coverage gate from advisory to required status.
*   **On-device inference (related to #8848):** Extensive discussion regarding the implementation of fused QJL/TBQ kernels. Findings indicate that current shipped models (head_dim=256) are incompatible with existing head_dim=128 kernels. Flash Attention (FA) remains disabled on Android due to non-deterministic races in the Mali GPU kernel, with focus shifting toward fused kernels and MTP speculative decoding as the primary performance path.
